text: To set effective project goals, start by making them SMART: Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of “improve the website,” say “increase sign-ups by 20% in three months.” Next, break the work into milestones, like completing the design phase or testing the new features. Each milestone should lead to a clear deliverable, such as a report or a finished prototype. These checkpoints help you track progress and adjust if needed. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment—discuss goals with everyone involved, from team members to clients. When all parties agree on the objectives, the project runs smoother and avoids confusion later.
